answer:
Sure! So, there was this time when I really wasn’t into history at all. It all changed when my friend Sarah, who’s super passionate about it, invited me to a documentary screening about the American Civil Rights Movement. At first, I was like, “Meh, not really my thing,” but I decided to go, just to hang out with her.

During the screening, I was honestly blown away by the powerful stories of courage and change. Sarah had this way of explaining the events that made them come alive for me. She shared personal anecdotes about her grandparents living through that era, which added a whole new layer of understanding. It felt so real, you know? Afterwards, we ended up chatting for hours about civil rights leaders and their impact. 

That experience really opened my eyes. I started reading books and watching more documentaries on my own. I learned not just about historical facts, but also about the importance of standing up for what’s right. It was such an eye-opener and definitely sparked a passion in me that I never expected to have. Thanks to Sarah, history went from being this boring subject to something I genuinely care about.
title:
Describe a time when you felt encouraged to learn about something you previously were not interested in.
What did you learn about?
Who encouraged you?
How did they spark your interest in the new subject?
